member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient
member.summary: `IRelayClient` is a transport layer. All it is responsible for is wrapping buffers of data in appropriate packets / headers so that they can be addressed to the right players. It also handles player ID assignment. It does NOT handle any protocol specific logic. In particular, it should know nothing of: 1) specific RPC calls 2) ECS components and their state. This is important because: 1) There will be many implementations of `IRelayClient` and we want to keep these implementations minimal and with as little duplication as possible. 2) Relay client cannot be responsible for the entire protocol anyway. Therefore, by the principle of single responsibility it cannot be responsible for just part of the protocol. It would create a confusion as to which parts of the protocol belong where exactly. Over time this would lead to a difficult to maintain code. `IRelayClient` has to know about areas of interest, because these are necessary to handle routing efficiently. In particular, for most use-cases we don't want send messages using a recipient list but rather using the area of interest. `IRelayClient` also has to know of entity ownership, at least to the extent that this feature is used for addressing messages. In particular, one of the addressing modes allows us to indicate that the entity should be relayed to the owner of the entity. This owner cannot be pre-concretized because while the message is in flight, ownership could be transferred to another player. Retaining the owner addressing mode allows the old owner to re-relay the message to the new owner in that specific rare case.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnStart
member.summary: Fired immediately after the client requests connection to the server. The client is not yet connected when this is fired. Always called from the thread calling `Start()`.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnRequestedStop
member.summary: Fired immediately after the client requests disconnection from the server. This will NOT fire if the client got disconnected from the server unwillingly, e.g. due to a network error. Always called from the MAIN thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnConnected
member.summary: Fired when the client-server handshake got completed and the client is able to receive and send messages to/from the server. Before the `OnConnected` event `IRelayClient` guarantees that it will not fire any events related to messages from the server. Sending messages to the server before the `OnConnected` event fires will result in an exception. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnDisconnected
member.summary: Fired when an expected or unexpected disconnection from the server. After this event is fired, no further events related to messages from the server will be fired untile successful reconnection. Similarly, attempting to send messages in the disconnected state will result in an exception. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnOtherPlayerConnected
member.summary: Fired when another player has connected to the server. This will fire for all players regardless of their area of interest. This is so that we can enumerate all players regardless of where we are in the game, e.g. show statistics about the total number of players on the server. Or to be able to send whisper messages to players in different areas of interest. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnOtherPlayerDisconnected
member.summary: Fired when another player has been disconnected from the server. This is the counterpart to `OnOtherPlayerConnected`. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnRequestedJoinArea
member.summary: Fired immediately after the client requests to join an area of interest. The client is not yet in the area of interest when this is fired. Always called from the MAIN thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnJoinedArea
member.summary: Fired when the client has successfully joined an area of interest. Before this event is fired, the client will not receive any messages addressed to the area of interest. The client will always leave an area of interest before joining a new one. It is currently impossible to be in multiple areas of interest at the same time. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnRequestedLeaveArea
member.summary: Fired immediately after the client requests to leave an area of interest. The client has not yet left the area of interest when this is fired. In particular, it is possible to still receive messages addressed to the "stale" area of interest before the `OnLeftArea` event is fired. Always called from the MAIN thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnLeftArea
member.summary: Fired when the client has successfully left an area of interest. Before joining another area of interest, the client will not receive any area of interest addressed messages. The client is considered to be in no area of interest at this point (similarly to when it first gets connected to the server). Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnOtherPlayerJoinedArea
member.summary: Fired when another player joins our area of interest. We are not going to be informed about players joining areas of interest where we ourselves are not joined. When we join a new area of interest, this event will fire for each player already in that area of interest. If a new player joins the area of interest, this will fire as well. We will always first be informed of a new player connecting to the server before we get informed about it joining our area of interest. Even when dealing with a reconnection. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnOtherPlayerLeftArea
member.summary: Counterpart to `OnOtherPlayerJoinedArea`. Fires when another player leaves our area of interest. This will also fire for players that were in the area of interest and got disconnected. This will fire before the `OnOtherPlayerDisconnected` event in that case. When leaving an area of interest, we will receive this event for each player that was in the area of interest before we left and that we knew of. Also, will be fired if a player gets disconnected or if they leave the area of interest on request. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnPingUpdated
member.summary: Used to measure ping. Currently only measures round-trip ping to the server. Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnAnyBuiltInMessage
member.summary: Fired for each message successfully received by us. The following messages will be received: 1) Directly addressed to us. 2) Address to all players in the area of interest where we are. 3) Global messages send to everyone on the server regardless of area of interest. TODO: Rate limiting? Always called from the same NETWORK thread.

member.name: E:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClient.OnClientUpdate
member.summary: Fired on each server update tick. This is called from the thread calling `RunAsync()`.

member.name: P:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRelayClientNetworkThreadContext.IsConnected
member.summary: Whether the client is currently connected to the server - including having a valid `PlayerId` assigned. If the client disconnects as a result of a network error, this will be set to `false`, while `IsRunning` will remain `true` until `Stop()` is called.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.RelayMessage.ToPeers(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMessageCode,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId[],LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod)
member.summary: Send an event to a specific player or group of players. This overload does not support event caching, as cached events must either be sent to all other players or all players.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.RelayMessage.ByRelayMode(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMessageCode,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId,ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode,LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod)
member.summary: Send an event with a specific delivery method. This overload does not support event caching.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.ECS.Systems.SendComponentDeltaSystemBase`1.GetMaxPacketSize
member.returns: null if unbound, otherwise the max packet size in bytes

members.member: Returns a bitmask of player IDs to send to. Bit 0 = PlayerId 0, Bit 1 = PlayerId 1, etc. This in an optimization to avoid allocating HashSets every tick. This replaces the old bool OwnsEntity(_) check.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Generators.DeriveINetSerializableAttribute
member.summary: Decorate a struct with this attribute to make it available for use as an RPC parameter.
member.param: Determines which members of the struct are serialized. See for details.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Generators.DeriveINetSerializableAttribute.#ctor(ReadyM.Api.Serialization.SerializableMode)
member.summary: Decorate a struct with this attribute to make it available for use as an RPC parameter.
member.param: Determines which members of the struct are serialized. See for details.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Generators.RpcEventAttribute
member.summary: Marks a method as an RPC event handler. Use this in partial classes extending from .
member.param: Determines how the event is relayed to other clients.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Generators.RpcEventAttribute.#ctor(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode)
member.summary: Marks a method as an RPC event handler. Use this in partial classes extending from .
member.param: Determines how the event is relayed to other clients.

members.member: Propagate the event to both the ECS and the game, regardless of the policy.
Propagate the event to the ECS if the event policy allows it. Whether the event was propagated.
Propagate the event to the game if the event policy allows it. Whether the event was propagated.
Should data be copied from the game object to the ECS entity
Should data be copied from the ECS entity to the game object
Should data be allowed to be set from the API.
Should data be allowed to be stored / set locally (analogous to ShouldRunLocally for events).
Used with events that are only happening on the game-side and it makes no sense to trigger them manually.
Used with events that are only happening on the game-side and it makes no sense to trigger them manually.
Used for events that are only sent to clients from the server and therefore are never triggered locally.
Used for events that are only sent to clients from the server and therefore are never triggered locally.
Always returns false if the event is already propagating to the game, to avoid recursion.
Returns whether the event should be allowed to propagate to the ECS or not, based on the provided context. This is usually called in patches to game code.
Always returns false if the event is already propagating to the ECS, to avoid recursion.
Returns whether the event should be allowed to propagate to the game or not, based on the provided context. This is usually called in network event handlers.

Returns whether the game is allowed to run the game code related to the event locally, or not. This is used exclusively in game code patches, to avoid running events for entities we are not responsible for. Always returns true if the event is propagating from the ECS to the game, to allow the game code to run for the event.

Returns whether the game is allowed to run the game code related to the event locally, or not. This is used exclusively in game code patches, to avoid running events for entities we are not responsible for.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.CustomRelayEventHeader
member.summary: Spans 4 bytes in standard relay modes and (6 + 2 * peers) in peer-targeted relay mode. - 1 byte for eventCode - 2 bytes for sender - 1 byte for relayMode and eventCaching (bit packed) - 2 bytes for peers count - peers * 2 bytes for peers

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.CustomRelayEventHeader.#ctor(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMessageCode,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId[],ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode)
member.summary: Spans 4 bytes in standard relay modes and (6 + 2 * peers) in peer-targeted relay mode. - 1 byte for eventCode - 2 bytes for sender - 1 byte for relayMode and eventCaching (bit packed) - 2 bytes for peers count - peers * 2 bytes for peers

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMessageCode
member.summary: A unique identifier of a single RPC message type.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode
member.summary: Indicates how an RPC message should be relayed to other players in the game.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.AreaOfInterestOthers
member.summary: Sends the message to all players in the area of interest of the sender, other than the sender.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.AreaOfInterestAll
member.summary: Sends the message to all players in the area of interest of the sender, including the sender.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.GlobalOthers
member.summary: Sends the message to all players in the game, other than the sender.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.GlobalAll
member.summary: Sends the message to all players in the game, including the sender.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.EntityOwner
member.summary: Sends the message to the owner of an entity, possibly back to the sender.

member.name: F:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMode.Peers
member.summary: Sends the message to a specific list of players.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.ServerEventHeader
member.summary: Spans 3 bytes. - 1 byte for eventCode - 2 bytes for sender

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.ServerEventHeader.#ctor(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Protocol.Enums.RelayMessageCode,ReadyM.Api.Idents.PlayerId)
member.summary: Spans 3 bytes. - 1 byte for eventCode - 2 bytes for sender

member.name: T:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase
member.summary: Base class for an RPC container. Each class that defines RPC methods with [RpcEvent] attributes must inherit from this class.
member.param: RPC client to use for sending RPCs. This is injected by the DI container.
Serializer to use for serializing RPC parameters. This is injected by the DI container.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.#ctor(ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Client.IRpcClient,ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Serialization.IRelaySerializer)
member.summary: Base class for an RPC container. Each class that defines RPC methods with [RpcEvent] attributes must inherit from this class.
member.param: RPC client to use for sending RPCs. This is injected by the DI container.
Serializer to use for serializing RPC parameters. This is injected by the DI container.

member.name: P:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.EventsCount
member.summary: Number of RPC events defined in this class. Generated by the source generator in derived classes.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.InitRpc
member.summary: Initializes the RPC class, e.g. by registering RPC handlers. Generated by the source generator in derived classes.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.DeInitRpc
member.summary: De-initializes the RPC class, e.g. by unregistering RPC handlers. Generated by the source generator in derived classes.

member.name: P:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.Offset
member.summary: Event code offset assigned to this RPC class. Generated by the source generator in derived classes.

member.name: P:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.RelayClient
member.summary: RPC client to use for sending RPCs. This is injected by the DI container.

member.name: P:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.RPC.RpcClassBase.Serializer
member.summary: Serializer to use for serializing RPC parameters. This is injected by the DI container.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Serialization.RelaySerializer.SerializeObject(LiteNetLib.Utils.NetDataWriter,System.Object)
member.summary: Writes the object to the stream. Format: - PlayerId size (2 bytes) - byte type code (1 byte) - data

member.name: M:ReadyM.Api.Multiplayer.Serialization.RelaySerializer.DeserializeObject(LiteNetLib.Utils.NetDataReader)
member.summary: Deserializes the object from the stream. The data must be in the same format as serialized by SerializeObject.
